statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 19 of 27 countries facing critical shortages in at least one fuel type, despite overall reserves near mandated minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as crude oil prices surge to $101.43 per barrel, up 6.54% and approximately 27% since the current Middle East conflict began. While EU-wide averages for petrol (94.5 days) and diesel (95.8 days) remain just above the mandatory 90-day minimum, jet fuel reserves have fallen to a concerning 89.9 days, dropping below required thresholds.

The situation varies dramatically across member states. Romania, Poland, and Spain face the most severe challenges, with all three fuel types in critical status. Romania shows particularly alarming diesel reserves at just 20.2 days, while Poland's jet fuel stocks have dropped to 21.3 days. In total, 19 of 27 EU countries report critical shortages in at least one fuel category, indicating widespread strain following recent strategic reserve releases during Middle East supply disruptions.

Price pressures are mounting alongside supply concerns, with diesel averaging €2.00 per liter and significant variation between member states. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol costs at €2.334 per liter, while Malta enjoys the lowest at €1.34 per liter. This price divergence, combined with uneven reserve distribution, suggests the EU's fuel security challenges extend beyond simple supply volumes to include affordability and equitable access across the bloc.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries report critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with Romania, Poland, and Spain most severely affected across all fuel types
EU jet fuel reserves at 89.9 days have fallen below the mandatory 90-day minimum for net imports
Crude oil prices have surged 27% since the Middle East conflict began, reaching $101.43 per barrel with continued upward pressure
Significant price disparities exist across member states, with Netherlands petrol costs 74% higher than Malta's
Romania's diesel reserves at 20.2 days and Poland's jet fuel at 21.3 days represent the most critical individual shortages in the dataset
